memory.summary: I can finish this assignment in minutes and submit it before the 13:30 deadline. With no other urgent tasks, I should just lock this in immediately.
The missing task file is a blocker I can't ignore; finding it now or renegotiating the deadline directly beats wasting time guessing. My focus drops if I keep circling this uncertainty.
I missed the deadline by seconds despite being 95% done, which hurts my grade more than wasting time searching. I need to stop overthinking and just submit what I have now.

desires: Submit this assignment immediately to stop the bleeding on my GPA and prove I can actually deliver before a deadline.
Force myself to ignore the low-priority note tidying so I don't waste another second on soft tasks while my hard deadline is ticking.

goals.rationale: My achievement drive screams for closure on this open loop. My impulsive nature makes the ticking clock unbearable, so I'm cutting my losses rather than obsessing over that 5% or searching for files that might not exist. Perfectism is procrastination, and I need the win.
